7296.built_by: Devonport Dockyard (Plymouth, U.K.) : Vickers Armstrong (Newcastle-on-Tyne, U.K.) (diesels engines only)
7296.history: Former cruiser minelayer. Became a repair ship in March 1944. Sold 10 July 1947.

commanders: A/Capt. (retired) James Halliburton Young, RN (30 Aug 1944 - mid 1946)
A/Capt. (retired) Edmund Hugh Hopkinson, RN (Nov 1944 - 6 Jan 1946)

7302.built_by: Swan Hunter and Wigham Richardson Ltd. (Wallsend-on-Tyne, U.K.)
7302.history: Requisitioned by the Admiralty while building in June 1940. Displacement: 9847 tons. Lenght: 503.5 feet Machinery: Two shafts, Diesel engine, BHP 7500 = 16.5 knots. Armament: 2 4" AA guns (2x1), 2 2 pdr AA (2x1), 4 20mm AA (4x1), 550 mines. Burned out at Loch Alsh on 27 November 1940
commanders: Capt. (retired) John Norman Tait, DSC, RN (1 May 1940 - 27 Nov 1940)

7303.built_by: J. Readhead & Sons Ltd. (South Shields, U.K.)
7303.history: Laid down as Empire Pitcairn, completed as HMS Moray Firth, aircraft engine repair shipy. HMS Moray Firth was intended as a repair ship for the maintenance and overhaul of Rolls Royce "Merlin" aero-engines. 1947 sold to Stag Line Ltd, converted into a cargo ship. 1948 renamed Linaria by same owners. 1954 sold to Chellew Nav. Co. Ltd renamed Eskglen. 1956 Managers restyled as Esk Shipping Co.Ltd. 1961 sold to, Fortune Shipping Co, Hong Kong renamed Marine Fortune, 1967 Scrapped Yokosuka, Japan.
commanders: William Thomas Fitzgerald, RNR (16 Jul 1945 - mid 1946)
Lt.Cdr. Gilliat Benfield Herbert-Jones, RD, RNR (14 Mar 1945 - mid 1946)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. Alexander Albert Davidson, RNR (1 Jan 1945 - 7 May 1945)
John Emrys Evans, RNR (7 May 1945 - mid 1946)

D M McLean, RNR (9 Jul 1945 - 1 Aug 1945)
A/Cdr. (retired) Leslie Rodney Sharp, RD, RNR (1 Aug 1945 - late 1945)
A/Cdr. William Snowdon Byles, RD, RNR (Mar 1945 - 10 Oct 1945)
A/Capt. Malcolm Farquhar, RN (18 Jul 1945 - late 1945)

7331.history: HrMs Kortenaer (Lt.Cdr. Antonie Kroese) was sunk by a Japanese torpedo, most likely from the heavy cruiser Haguro (offsite link), during the Battle of the Java Sea in position 06º29'S, 112º05'E. Some 60 of her crew were killed in this action. See also this website (offsite link).
commanders: luitenant ter zee 1e klasse (Lt.Cdr.) Lambert Johan Goslings, RNN (26 Mar 1940 - 30 Sep 1940)
luitenant ter zee 1e klasse (Lt.Cdr.) Antonie Kroese, RNN (30 Sep 1940 - 27 Feb 1942)

7332.history: Van Galen served along her sister ships in the Netherlands East Indies until 1939, when she was ordered back to Holland. She arrived in Den Helder on 8 May 1940 only two days before the Germans attack. On the 10th HrMs Van Galen (Cdr. Albertus Samuel Pinke) was ordered to shell the German forces at the captured airfield Waalhaven near Rotterdam. Before she could do so she was attacked by German aircraft and badly damaged. Nevertheless she made it to the Merwedehaven, where she finally sank. The ship was raised by the Germans on 23 October 1941, and being not worth repairing she was scrapped in the Netherlands in Hendrik Ido Ambacht. See also this website (offsite link).
commanders: kapitein-luitenant ter zee (Cdr.) Albertus Samuel Pinke, RNN (26 Mar 1940 - 11 May 1940)

7333.built_by: Koninklijke Maatschappij De Schelde (Vlissingen (Flushing), The Netherlands)
7333.history: HrMs G 13 managed to escape to the U.K. Transferred to the Royal Navy on 9 August 1940. Returned to the Royal Natherlands navy on 27 July 1942. Scrapped in February 1943.
commanders: luitenant ter zee 1e klasse (Lt.Cdr.) Everhard Coenraad Wessels, RNN (19 Feb 1940 - 17 Jul 1940)
luitenant ter zee 1e klasse (Lt.Cdr.) Nicolaas Wilhelm Sluijter, RNN (17 Jul 1940 - 14 Aug 1940)

7334.history: HrMs G 15 managed to escape to the U.K. Transferred to the Royal Navy on 27 December 1940. Returned to the Royal Natherlands navy in June 1942. Scrapped in February 1943.
commanders: luitenant ter zee 2e klasse (Lt.) Willem Adriaan Montanus, RNN (30 Oct 1939 - 1 Jan 1941)

7335.history: Scuttled at Den Helder to prevent her capture by the Germans on 14 May 1940. Raised by the Germans and commissioned by them as TFA 9.

7340.history: Sunk by German aircraft off Dunkirk on 1 June 1940.The wreck lies in 15 meters of water in position 51.04.54N, 02.27.30E.

7342.history: Saint Class tug HMS Saint Mabyn sold in 1926 and renamend Caroline Moller. Requisitioned by the Royal Navy during the Second World War. Sunk by German Motor Torpedo Boats off Cromer on 7 October 1942.

7343.history: On 28 December 1942 the British tug HMS St. Issey (Lt. J.H.W. Howe, RNR) was torpedoed and sunk by U-617 off Benghazi, Lybia in position 32º37'N, 20º22'E. Hit by U-boatSunk on 28 Dec 1942 by U-617 (Brandi).
commanders: Lt. John Henry West Howe, RNR (??? - 28 Dec 1942 (+))

7347.history: HMS St. Cyrus (Lt. Joseph Cordery, RNR) was mined and sunk off the Humber on 22 January 1941.
commanders: Lt. Joseph Cordery, RNR (??? - 22 Jan 1941)

7349.history: HMRT St. Breock (Skipper Joseph Jappy, RNR) was bombed and sunk by Japanese aircraft off Sumatra, Netherlands East Indies on 14 February 1942.

7352.history: HMRT St. Fagan (Lt.Cdr. George Henry Warren, MBE, RN) was sunk by German aircraft off Dunkirk on 1 June 1940.
commanders: Lt. George Henry Warren, RN (24 May 1937 - 1 Jun 1940)

7354.history: Scuttled at Palembang Harbor by Crew.

7355.history: Saint Class tug sold commercially after the First World War. Requisitioned by the Royal Navy during the Second World War.

7356.history: Saint Class tug sold commercially after the First World War. Requisitioned by the Royal Navy during the Second World War. Its offical number is 143909 Registered by the Admiralty in London. However she never appears on the Navy List and never went into service while in Admiralty ownership. Supplement 1925-25 she was transferrrrred to French ownership at that time and her full entry in Lloyds Register of Shipping in the 1926-27 edition reads ABEILLE No 22( -ex St. MINVER) During the war this vessel was requisitioned by the German Navy.

7357.history: Grounded and declared a total loss, off Duncansby Head, Scotland on 21 September 1942.

7359.history: HMS Buccaneer was sunk on 26 August 1946 by an accidental hit from a shell during target practice.

7360.history: Bandit was based in Simonstown, South Africa for part of the war. In 1947 she was renamed Briton. From 15 June until 29 June 1948 Briton was docked for the last time in the Selborne dry dock at Simonstown prior to her return to the U.K. In 1960 Briton was sold to commercial use.

7361.history: Sold and scrapped in Italy on 23 September 1960.

7362.history: Sold into mercantile service on 20 October 1958 and renamed Emerson K. Capsized at Capetown, South Africa on 10 August 1965 and scrapped there starting on 16 March 1966.
commanders: T/Lt. Walter James Hammond, RNR (24 Dec 1939 - 8 Nov 1941)
T/Lt. Frank Jennings, RNR (8 Nov 1941 - mid/late44)

7363.history: Sold on 30 October 1968 to Tsavliris Salvage & Towage Ltd at Piraeus, Greece and renamed Nisos Mykonos.

7365.history: HMRT Frisky was sold and renamed Hasan in 1948. Hasan was renamed Vernicos Marina in 1960.

7366.history: Renamed Tenacity in November 1940. Renamed Adherent in 1947. Sold into mercantile cervice in 1960 being renamed Hermes.

7367.history: HMRT Jaunty was sold on 21 December 21 1966.
commanders: T/Lt. William Sutton Lowrey, DSC, RNR (??? - Aug 1945)
T/Lt. Carlton Arthur Hire, RNR (Aug 1945 - 20 Jul 1946)

7368.history: HMRT Restive was sold on 11 June 1965.

7369.history: HMRT Allegiance was loaned to ??? in 1949 and renamed Allegiance II. She was renamed Kowloon docks in 1955. Foundered off Hong Kong on 1 September 1962.

7370.history: Grounded off Lough Foyle, Northern Ireland on 18 October 1941 and declared a total loss.

7371.history: HMRT Prudent was renamed Cautious when she became a Dockyard Tug in 1947. Sold and renamed Rivtow Lion in 1964. She was finally sunk as a recreational dive site at Nanaimo, BC (Canada) on 6 February 2005.

7372.history: Launched as Stormcock but renamed Stormking before commissioning. Became the dockyard tug Tryphon in 1947. Sold on 1 July 1957 and renamed Melanie Fair. Sold in 1961 to Imprese Maritime e Portuali in Genoa, Italy and renamed Toro. Broken for scrap in the spring of 1969.

7373.history: HMRT Prosperous was sold on 7 January 1965.

7374.history: HMRT Horsa (former Rescue, renamed Horsa in 1942) was wrecked on the east coast of Iceland near Osfles Rock on 16 March 1943.

7375.history: HMRT Adept was wrecked in the Hebrides area on 17 March 1942.

7376.history: HMRT Adherent (Lt. Stanley Wilfred Potter, RNR) foundered in the North Atlantic on 14 January 1944.
commanders: T/Lt. Stanley Wilfred Potter, RNR (??? - 14 Jan 1944)

7377.history: HMRT Charon was renamed Alligator when she became a Dockyard Tug in 1947. Scrapped at Tipnor in January 1959.

7378.history: HMRT Hengist (former Decision, renamed Hengist in 1941) was sold in 1965 and renamed Nisos Crete.

7379.history: HMRT Griper sold to the Singapore Harbour Board in December 1946. Griper was renamed Surabaja in 1962.

7380.history: HMRT Dexterous was sold and entered mercantile service on 23 January 1947. In 1957 she was renamed Zurmand.

7381.history: Sold on 1 March 1965 and renamed Nisos Chios.

7382.history: HMRT Antic (former name Ant, renamed Antic in 1942 before being launched). Manned by a civilian crew from the Netherlands from June 1943 until 1945. Became a Royal Navy Fleet Tug in 1946. Scrapped in November 1969.

7383.history: Sold in to mercantile service and renamed Irving Tamarack in September 1958.

7384.history: Operated by the Royal Fleet Auxiliary. Earner (former Earnest, renamed Earner in 1943) was sold and renamed Nisos Rodos in January 1965.

7385.history: Torpedoed and sunk by a German Motor Torpedo Boat off Normandy on 11 June 1944.

7386.history: In 1947 Samasonia was loaned under long-term charter to the Canadian Foundation Maritime Company and renamed Foundation Josephine.

7389.history: Grounded in the Mediterranean off Libya on 9 February 1945.

7391.history: Sunk in a collision in thick fog in the River Forth on 10 August 1976. She was raised and broken up.

7392.history: This ship became famous for the unsuccesfull attempt to salvage the US Merchant Flying Enterprise in January 1952.

7400.history: Returned to the US Navy on 19 February 1946

7401.history: Returned to the US Navy in February 1946 Sold into mercantile service during 1946 and renamed Ming 308

7402.history: Returned to the US Navy in March 1946 Sold into mercantile service in 1948 and renamed Eugenia M. Moran Renamed Comanche in 1960

commanders: T/Lt. Douglas Jack Strath, RANR(S) (14 Oct 1944 - )

7405.built_by: Defoe Shipbuilding Co. (Bay City, Michigan, U.S.A.)
7405.history: Returned to the US Navy in 1946 Sold on 14 March 1948

7406.built_by: Defoe Shipbuilding Co. (Bay City, Michigan, U.S.A.)
7406.history: Returned to the US Navy on 13 June 1946 Sold on 2 October 1948

7407.built_by: Defoe Shipbuilding Co. (Bay City, Michigan, U.S.A.)
7407.history: Returned to the US Navy in 1946 Sold into mercantile service in 1948 being renamed Frosty Moller Renamed Christine Moller in 1950 Oceanus in 1951 and Gele Zee in 1953

7408.built_by: Defoe Shipbuilding Co. (Bay City, Michigan, U.S.A.)
7408.history: Returned to the US Navy in 1946 Sold into mercantile service in 1946 being renamed Ming 305

7409.built_by: Gulfport Shipbuilding Co. (Port Arthur, Texas, U.S.A.)
7409.history: Returned to the US Navy in 1946 and sold into mercantile service being renamed Ocean Pride Renamed Pan America in 1947 Renamed Zeeland in 1956

7411.history: Returned to the US Navy on 27 March 1946 and sold into mercantile service in 1946 being renamed Susan A. Moran Renamed Eugene F. Moran later in 1946 and Monsanta in 1947

7412.built_by: Gulfport Shipbuilding Co. (Port Arthur, Texas, U.S.A.)

7414.built_by: Livingston Ship Building Co. (Orange, Texas, U.S.A.)
7414.history: Returned to the United States Navy on 20 March 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 1 May 1946.

commanders: T/Lt. Gordon Alexander Keith, RANR(S) (27 Aug 1943 - 11 Mar 1944)
T/Lt. Donald Morrison, RANR(S) (11 Mar 1944 - )

7418.history: Mined and sunk off Leghorn, Italy on 17 July 1945.
